studies.company_provider: GET.ON Institut für Online Gesundheitstrainings GmbH
studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.follow_up_after_primary_end_point: Yes; uncontrolled follow-up at 6 and 12 months after randomisation
studies.duration_additional_info: Primary endpoint assessed at 3 months (post-treatment) after randomisation. Uncontrolled follow-up assessments for both intervention and control groups at 6 and 12 months after randomisation. Control group participants receive free access to the online programme after the 3-month data collection period if they wish.
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Intervention group (IG): direct access to HelloBetter ratiopharm chronic pain digital therapy programme (7 units, ACT-based); Control group (CG): care-as-usual (CAU) waiting control group with unrestricted access to routine care. Total planned and enrolled: 360 participants.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Arm 1 (Intervention): Immediate access to the web-based ACT programme HelloBetter ratiopharm chronic pain (7 units), assessed at baseline, post (3 months), and follow-up at 6 and 12 months after randomisation. Arm 2 (Control): Care-as-usual (CAU) waiting control group with unrestricted access to all routine care options (GP, pain physicians, psychotherapy, medication, physical therapy, exercise therapy); assessed at baseline, 3, 6, and 12 months after randomisation. After 3-month data collection, control participants may receive free access to the programme on request.
studies.collected_data: Primary outcome: Pain interference measured by the West Haven-Yale Multidimensional Pain Inventory (MPI) interference scale at post-treatment (3 months after randomisation). Secondary outcomes (collected at baseline, post/3 months, 6-month and 12-month follow-up unless noted): Emotional functioning (PHQ-9); Health-related quality of life (AQoL-8D); Pain intensity and interference (Brief Pain Inventory, BPI); Multidimensional pain interference including pain intensity, self-control, negative mood (MPI); Pain impairment in everyday life (Pain Disability Index, PDI); Anxiety (GAD-7); Pain processing (FESV); Pain acceptance (Chronic Pain Acceptance Questionnaire, CPAQ); Psychological flexibility (FAH-II / AAQ-II; Psychological Inflexibility in Pain Scale, PIPS); Pain-specific self-efficacy (Pain Self-Efficacy Questionnaire, PSEQ); Patient satisfaction (CSQ-8/ZUF-8, post, IG only); Negative effects (NEQ, post, IG only); Subjective global assessment of change (PGIC, post).

studies.company_provider: GET.ON Institut für Online Gesundheitstrainings GmbH
studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: The study includes 3 visits per patient: baseline (V1), telephone visit after 4 weeks (V2, intervention group only), and a visit at month 3 (V3, all patients). The primary endpoint is assessed at month 3 (approximately 12 weeks). The online course (HelloBetter app) consists of seven weekly units of 45-60 minutes each.
studies.intervention_and_control_group: 1:1 randomization; total enrolled: 136 (planned: 250). Group 1 Intervention: axSpA patients prescribed the HelloBetter ACT-app in addition to standard therapy. Group 2 Control: axSpA patients receiving standard therapy without the HelloBetter app. Exact per-group sizes not reported.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Arm 1 (Intervention): Patients with axSpA who continue to suffer from pain despite adequate anti-inflammatory medication are prescribed the digital health application (DiGA) 'HelloBetter ratiopharm chronic pain®' (HelloBetter ACT-app), an interactive online behavioral therapy/ACT program consisting of seven weekly units of 45-60 minutes. Visits: V1 (baseline), V2 (telephone visit at 4 weeks to verify app activation), V3 (month 3). Arm 2 (Control): Patients with axSpA who continue to suffer from pain despite adequate anti-inflammatory medication receive standard therapy without the HelloBetter app. Visits: V1 (baseline) and V3 (month 3).
studies.collected_data: Primary outcome: Change in the pain interference scale of the West Haven-Yale Multidimensional Pain Inventory (MPI-D) at month 3. Secondary outcomes and instruments collected at baseline (V1) and month 3 (V3): NRS global pain (0-10), back pain (BASDAI question 2), nocturnal back pain, inflammatory back pain (BASDAI questions 5 and 6), morning stiffness (BASDAI questions 5 and 6), West Haven-Yale Multidimensional Pain Inventory (MPI-D), neuropathic pain (painDetect questionnaire), PASS (patient acceptable symptom state), Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale (HADS), Ankylosing Spondylitis Disease Activity Score (ASDAS), Bath Ankylosing Spondylitis Disease Activity Index (BASDAI), Bath Ankylosing Spondylitis Functional Index (BASFI), Bath Ankylosing Spondylitis Metrology Index (BASMI), modified Short Questionnaire to Assess Health-enhancing Physical Activity (mSQUASH), ASAS Health Index (ASAS HI), Fibromyalgia Impact Questionnaire (FIQ), Short Form 36 (SF36), patient and physician global assessment, Patient Activation Measure (PAM12), patient global impression of change (V3 only). Intervention group only at V3: benefit from app use (yes/no), recommendation likelihood (NRS 0-10), Mobile Application Rating Scale (MARS), system usability questionnaire. V2 (intervention group only): app activation status (dichotomous yes/no). Demographic characteristics including educational and employment status, current pharmacological and non-pharmacological therapy.

studies.company_provider: GET.ON Institut für Online Gesundheitstrainings GmbH
studies.risk_class: Risk Class I according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: T0 = before randomisation, T1 = 9 weeks after randomisation (post-intervention), T2 = 6 months after randomisation (follow-up). The intervention consists of 7 lessons to be processed weekly.
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Three arms: (1) GET.ON chronic pain guided (with e-coach feedback emails after each lesson), (2) GET.ON chronic pain unguided (no e-coach feedback, self-directed), (3) waitlist control group. Total planned: 300, total enrolled: 302.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Arm 1: Guided internet-based ACT intervention (GET.ON chronic pain guided) - 7 weekly lessons with feedback emails from an e-coach after each lesson, plus optional SMS coach. Arm 2: Unguided internet-based ACT intervention (GET.ON chronic pain unguided) - same 7 weekly lessons but without e-coach feedback, plus optional SMS coach. Arm 3: Waitlist control group - receives no active intervention during the study period.
studies.collected_data: Primary outcome: Pain interference measured by the MPI Interference Scale at T0 (pre), T1 (9 weeks post-randomisation), and T2 (6 months post-randomisation). Secondary outcomes: At screening - demographic variables (sex, age, education, social support), prior pain treatments, pain type, chronicity, comorbidities (self-report). At T0, T1, T2 - Brief Pain Inventory (BPI), Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ-9), Generalised Anxiety Disorder Screener (GAD-7), Pain intensity (11-point NRS), Quality of life: SF-12 and EQ-5D, Acceptance and Action Questionnaire-II (AAQ-II / FAH-II), Chronic Pain Acceptance Questionnaire (CPAQ-D), adapted TiC-P (costs associated with psychiatric illness). Treatment process (after lessons 2, 4, 6): FAH-II and CPAQ-D. At T1 and T2: Patient Global Impression of Change scale (PGIC). Active groups only at T1: intervention adherence (attrition rate), satisfaction with intervention (CSQ-8 / ZUF-8).

audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results

study)

audit.study_title:

a three-armed randomised controlled trial
audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results
